title: Cas9-targeted enrichment of MEIs for ONT R9 flow cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f273g","text":"This is the Boyle lab's Cas9-targeted enrichment for mobile element insertions within genomic DNA isolated from cell lines. We use ONT R9 flow cells for long-read sequencing following Cas9 enrichment. Based on our published L1H enrichment, we expect 50x or greater coverage over the targeted MEI.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3qhf8","text":"Nuclei Lysis Buffer solution:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vtej","text":"10mM Tris-HCl, pH 8","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1gpso","text":"400mM NaCl","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"g2k7","text":"2mM EDTA, pH 8","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d9qs6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1a0in","text":"EnGen sgRNA Synthesis Kit (NEB, E3322S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4l2js","text":"Molecular biology grade water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"99s4k","text":"Qubit instrument w/dsDNA BR and HS assay kit; RNA BR assay kit","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bork1","text":"Alt-R S.p. Cas9 HiFi V3 nuclease (IDT)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3h4tk","text":"10X Cutsmart Buffer (NEB, can also use rCutsmart Buffer without any issues)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2dtit","text":"Taq DNA polymerase (NEB, M0273L)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"do6v5","text":"10mM dATP","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2vmuo","text":"T4 DNA ligase (NEB, M0202M)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8dgg7","text":"SQK-LSK109 kit from Oxford Nanopore Technologies","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"98q2p","text":"MinION R9 flow cell","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cbeoe","text":"MinION sequencer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4tjj1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Pellet cells in swinging bucket centrifuge at 500 x g, 4 °C, 00:10:00
Remove supernant
Wash cell pellets in cold 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline )
go to step #1
Add 5 mL of cold 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline ) to cell pellet and resuspend the cells
Count cells using your method of choice; we used a Countess FL
Move forward with the number of cells you need - We recommend at least 20M cells
go to step #1
Resuspend the cell pellet in 3 mL of nuclei lysis buffer
Add 200 µL of SDS, 10% Solution Life Technologies Catalog #AM9822 to the resuspension and invert to mix evenly
Add 50 µL of RNase A Qiagen Catalog #19101
Rotate for 00:30:00 at 37 °C
Add 50 µL of 10 mg/mL Proteinase K Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #3115879001
Rotate Overnight at 37 °C
Add 1 mL of Sodium Chloride, 5 M Contributed by users
Shake by hand until evenly mixed
4000 x g, Room temperature, 15 minutes
Transfer supernant to new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube
Add 2 volumes of 100% Ethanol Contributed by users
Invert tube to mix until white strings (DNA) begin to aggregate together (approximately 20 times)
Try to remove the DNA via spooling with a sterile p10 tip and place into a new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube
If you are unable to remove the DNA with a tip, you can 10000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes to pellet the DNA
Remove ethanol and air dry pellet on the benchtop for 00:05:00
Resuspend DNA in at least 200 µL of 1X TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 1 mM EDTA) Contributed by users buffer 4 °C Overnight

If the DNA has the consistency of gelatin after the overnight incubation, you will need to add an addition 200-500uL of 1X TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 1 mM EDTA) Contributed by users to get the DNA to go into solution.

Quantify the DNA concentration using your method of choice; we recommend using a Qubit and taking 3 separate readings within the tube to get an average concentration: 1 from the top, 1 from the middle and 1 from the bottom.

Lyophilized custom DNA oligos for the specific guide RNA (gRNA) were purchased from IDT or Sigma Aldrich and resuspended to 100 micromolar (µM) . A 1:10 dilution was used as a working stock for in vitro transcription.

in vitro transcription of a gRNA was setup as follows using the EnGen sgRNA Synthesis Kit (NEB, E3322S) in a PCR tube: 10 µL, 2x EnGen sgRNA Reaction Mix 0.5 µL, 100mM DTT 2.5 µL, 1:10 custom DNA oligo 2 µL, EnGen sgnRNA enzyme mix 5 µL, molecular biology grade water
Incubate 37 °C 01:00:00
Add 30 µL, molecular biology grade water to bring the sample to 50uL
Add 2 µL, DNase to degrade remaining DNA oligos within the sample
Incubate 37 °C 00:15:00
Add 200 µL TRIzol Reagent Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15596026 and 50 µL Chloroform Contributed by users to the sample.
Move the in vitro transcribed sgRNA from the PCR tube into a 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube
Vortex for 00:00:10 to mix thoroughly
Centrifuge 20000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes

Remove the aqueous layer (should be the top clear layer) carefully, avoiding the middle (should be barely able to see if at all) and bottom (pink) layer, and transfer to a new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube.
Add 50 µL Chloroform Contributed by users
Vortex for 00:00:05 to mix
Centrifuge 20000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Remove the top aqueous layer and place in a new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube
Add 2 volumes of 100% Ethanol Contributed by users and 3M Sodium Acetate solution Contributed by users to a final concentration of 0.3M
Invert to mix
Centrifuge 20000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes
Remove the supernant being careful not to disturb the white pellet at the bottom of the tube
Wash the RNA pellet with 250 µL, 70% ethanol
Centrifuge 20000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes
Repeat 70% ethanol wash and centrifugation step
Remove as much residual ethanol as possible from the RNA pellet
Air dry on benchtop for 00:05:00
Resuspend pellet in 10 µL, RNase-free water
Quantify the RNA concentration using your method of choice, we recommend and use the Qubit

To perform enrichment for your specific MEI, be sure to use at least 5ug of HMW gDNA at a concentration of around 100ng/uL. For our L1H enrichment experiments, we used 10ug of gDNA. Add gDNA to a PCR tube and then the following to dephosphorylate the gDNA: 4 µL, 1X Cutsmart Buffer 6 µL, Quick CIP X µL, molecular grade water to 40uL total
Incubate in the thermocycler using the below protocol: 37 °C 00:30:00 80 °C 00:20:00
Cool the dephosphorylated gDNA sample on ice

While the gDNA is being dephosphorylated, make the Cas9 RNP using the mix below in a PCR tube: 850 ng, sgRNA 1 µL, 1:5 dilution of Cas9 protein 1.5 µL, 10X Cutsmart Buffer X µL, molecular biology grade water to 15uL total
Mix thoroughly by inversion
Incubate RNP Room temperature 00:20:00
Add 15 µL, RNP , 1 µL, Taq DNA polymerase and 1.5 µL, dATP to the gDNA sample tube
Mix by inversion or flicking
Incubate in a thermocycler using the below protocol: 37 °C 00:30:00 for Cas9 cleavage 72 °C 00:10:00 for a-tailing of cut ends
Allow the Cas9-enriched sample to cool on ice then move the sample into a 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube

Add the following from the SQK-LSK109 ONT ligation sequencing kit to the sample tube for sequencing adapter ligation: 25 µL, LNB 5 µL, AMX 5 µL, T4 DNA ligase (NEB, M0202M) 15 µL, molecular biology grade water for a total of 100uL
Mix by flicking then spin down briefly
Rotate Room temperature 00:30:00
Add 40 µL, Axygen magnetic beads from the SQK-LSK109 kit
Mix by inversion
Add 1 volume of 1X TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 1 mM EDTA) Contributed by users and mix by inversion
Rotate Room temperature 00:10:00 then Room temperature 00:05:00 without rotation
Place tube on magnetic rack and pellet the magnetic beads
Remove the supernant
Add 200 µL, LFB and resuspend by flicking to wash
Briefly spin down the beads and place on magnetic rack to pellet the beads
Repeat steps 65-67
Spin down the beads once more and place on the magnetic rack, remove as much of the residual LFB as possible
Add 16.8 µL, EB and resuspend the beads by flicking the tube
Incubate Room temperature 00:10:00
Place tube on magnetic rack and pellet the beads
Remove the elution and put into new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube, discard the tube with the beads
Quantify the library concentration using your method of choice, we recommend and use the Qubit
Prepare the MinION flow cell for sequencing following ONT's protocol
For loading the library onto the flow cell, add 26 µL, SQB to the library. We load as much library as we can onto the flow cell.
Immediately before loading onto the flow cell, add 0.5 µL, SQT and 9.5 µL, LB to the library.
Load the library onto the MinION following ONT's protocol

Make a 1:5 dilution of the Cas9 protein making the following mix in a PCR tube: 2.5 µL, 1X Cutsmart Buffer (make a 1X solution of the 10X buffer to use for this) 0.5 µL, Alt-R S.p. Cas9 HiFi V3 nuclease Mix by flicking the tube
creator: Jessica Switzenberg
